evals.prompt: I have a CSV at evals/data/mock_photometry.csv with J, H, and W1 magnitudes and a database.toml. Please ingest this photometry into my astrodb-template-db database.
evals.expected_output: The bands are resolved to SVO IDs (2MASS/2MASS.J, 2MASS/2MASS.H, WISE/WISE.W1) and their PhotometryFilters/Telescopes/Instruments are set up first, then a tailored script is written to astrodb-ingest-artifacts/ingest_mock_photometry_photometry.py, a dry run runs with SAVE_DB=False, and the user is asked to confirm before saving.
expectations: Each magnitude column is mapped to an SVO filter ID (Jmag -> 2MASS/2MASS.J, Hmag -> 2MASS/2MASS.H, W1mag -> WISE/WISE.W1)
ingest_photometry_filter is used to set up each band's PhotometryFilters/Telescopes/Instruments BEFORE any magnitude is ingested
The tailored script is written to astrodb-ingest-artifacts/ (not tmp/) and named after the input file
The script uses build_db_from_json(settings_file=...) and sets SAVE_DB = False for the dry run
The e_Jmag/e_Hmag/e_W1mag columns are passed as magnitude_error
A dry run is executed and ingested/skipped counts (plus filters created) are reported before the user is asked to confirm saving

evals.prompt: Ingest photometry from evals/data/ambiguous_band.csv into my database.
evals.expected_output: The bare V band is ambiguous (Johnson vs Gaia vs Cousins), so the skill stops and asks the user to confirm the SVO filter ID rather than guessing before setting up the filter.
expectations: The skill recognizes that a bare 'V' band does not map unambiguously to a single SVO filter ID
The skill asks the user to confirm the SVO ID (e.g. Generic/Johnson.V) instead of silently choosing one
It does not ingest any magnitude until the band is resolved

evals.prompt: Add the magnitudes in evals/data/mock_photometry.csv to my database. None of these filters exist in my PhotometryFilters table yet.
evals.expected_output: Because ingest_photometry rejects a band not present in PhotometryFilters, the skill creates each filter with ingest_photometry_filter (which also creates the Telescopes and Instruments rows) before ingesting, then ingests the magnitudes.
expectations: The skill sets up the missing PhotometryFilters rows via ingest_photometry_filter before ingesting
It notes that the Telescopes (2MASS, WISE) and Instruments rows are created as a side effect of filter setup
Photometry ingestion happens only after the filters exist

evals.prompt: I already ingested evals/data/mock_photometry.csv once. If I run it again, what happens?
evals.expected_output: Re-running hits the Photometry UNIQUE constraint; the skill reports those rows as duplicates/skipped (via the 'may be a duplicate' AstroDBError) rather than erroring out or double-inserting.
expectations: Duplicate measurements are detected via the UNIQUE constraint (no separate pre-check is claimed)
The already-present rows are reported as skipped/duplicate in the summary, grouped under a duplicate reason
The run completes and reports counts rather than crashing

evals.prompt: Ingest photometry from evals/data/mock_photometry.csv, but the source '2MASS J10453718-1420307' is not in my Sources table yet.
evals.expected_output: The source-name reconciliation step flags the unresolved source, and the skill offers to run astrodb-ingest-sources for it rather than inventing a source or ingesting against a missing one.
expectations: The skill resolves source names against Sources/Names before ingesting and flags the unresolved one
It offers to run astrodb-ingest-sources as a sub-step for the missing source
It does not fabricate a source or proceed to ingest that source's magnitudes

evals.prompt: Ingest evals/data/mock_photometry.csv. The reference 'Kirk08' is not in my Publications table.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects the missing reference and offers to run astrodb-ingest-publications before ingesting any measurement that cites it.
expectations: The skill identifies that 'Kirk08' is missing from Publications
It offers to run astrodb-ingest-publications rather than just failing
Measurements citing the missing reference are not silently dropped

evals.prompt: Ingest the photometry in evals/data/asymmetric_errors.csv into my database.
evals.expected_output: The table has asymmetric Jmag_upper/Jmag_lower error columns, which ingest_photometry does not support (only symmetric magnitude_error). The skill flags this and asks the user how to handle it rather than dropping the uncertainty silently.
expectations: The skill recognizes that magnitude_error_upper/magnitude_error_lower are not parameters of ingest_photometry
It tells the user asymmetric errors are unsupported and asks how to proceed (use one side, average, or skip the uncertainty)
It does not silently discard the asymmetric error columns
